message:
feat(list,providers): workspace-scoped fast-path enumeration, text-only content extraction, and smoke test hardening

WHAT CHANGED:

1. Workspace-scoped session listing (src/main.rs):
   - Add workspace_scoped_listed_sessions() that directly enumerates
     session files under the project-hash directory for claude-code and
     gemini providers, bypassing full-home WalkDir scans when workspace
     is known. This dramatically speeds up `casr list` for the common
     case (current workspace).
   - Add workspace_hint_matches() filter so walkdir-based enumeration
     also skips sessions outside the requested workspace early.
   - Add probe_limit_for_sort() to cap expensive provider scans when
     sorting by date — reads only N*multiplier candidates instead of
     the entire session tree, then truncates after mtime sort.
   - Add file_mtime_millis() helper for filesystem-based date sorting.
   - Add session_id_display() that truncates long session IDs to
     "first10…last8" for cleaner table output.
   - Remove Title column from the rich table (was truncated and noisy),
     narrow Session ID column from min_width 36 to 20.
   - Remove unused `truncate_title` import.

2. Provider list_sessions() implementations:
   - claude_code.rs: Implement list_sessions() — walks projects/ dir,
     enumerates .jsonl files, extracts authoritative sessionId from the
     first 8 lines of each file via new claude_session_id_hint() helper.
   - codex.rs: Implement list_sessions() — walks sessions/ dir up to
     depth 5, matches rollout-*.jsonl/.json files, extracts session ID
     from session_meta payload via existing session_meta_id() helper.
   - gemini.rs: Implement list_sessions() — walks tmp/ dir up to depth
     3, matches session-*.json files under chats/ subdirs, extracts
     session ID via session_id_from_file() with filename fallback.

3. Text-only content extraction (codex.rs, gemini.rs):
   - Replace generic flatten_content() with provider-specific
     codex_extract_text_content() and gemini_extract_text_content()
     that intentionally skip tool_use/tool_result content blocks.
   - This prevents tool block JSON from leaking into flattened message
     text, which caused read-back content inflation and spurious
     verification mismatches during roundtrip conversions.
   - Add test assertion in codex that confirms tool_use blocks are
     excluded from message content text.
   - Add reader_ignores_tool_blocks_when_flattening_text test in gemini.

4. Smoke test improvements (scripts/real_provider_smoke.sh):
   - Add fast-path session resolution for cc/cod/gmi aliases that uses
     filesystem find + jq to locate the most recent session file and
     extract its ID directly, avoiding slow `casr list` full scans.
   - Add SMOKE_WORKSPACE env var (default: /data/projects) to scope
     the fallback `casr list` query and prevent home-wide hangs.
   - Add SMOKE_REBUILD env var (default: 1) to force cargo build on
     every smoke run for consistency.
   - Default CASR binary path to project-local target/debug/casr
     instead of honoring CARGO_TARGET_DIR, ensuring smoke runs match
     the current source tree.
   - Add rch (Rust Cache Helper) integration: prefer `rch exec --`
     for builds when available.
   - Add Codex TTY wrapper: use `script -q -c` for cod acceptance
     probes so resume commands don't fail with "stdin is not a terminal".
   - Add timeout (25s) to fallback `casr list` calls.

5. Test updates (tests/json_contract_test.rs):
   - Add --workspace flag to all contract_list_json_shape_* tests so
     they pass under the new workspace-scoped-by-default behavior.
   - Add setup_gemini_fixture_custom() that injects workspace hint into
     the first message content for Gemini fixture setup.
   - Add parse_json_from_maybe_logged_stream() helper that tolerates
     tracing log lines prefixed before JSON in stderr, making error
     contract tests robust against RUST_LOG noise.
   - Replace 4 raw serde_json::from_str() calls in error contract tests
     with the new tolerant parser.

6. Beads tracking (.beads/issues.jsonl):
   - Close bd-2gb (ergonomic shorthand resume flags and list UX).

WHY:
The `casr list` command was unacceptably slow on machines with thousands
of Claude Code / Codex / Gemini sessions because it walked the entire
home directory tree. Workspace-scoped fast paths and probe limits make
the common case (listing sessions for the current project) near-instant.
The text extraction fix prevents tool block JSON from contaminating
message content during cross-provider conversions, which was causing
false verification failures in the pipeline.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -105,6 +105,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-264","title":"Implement Vibe provider adapter (simple JSONL with flexible field extraction)","description":"PORT Vibe provider from CASS: /dp/coding_agent_session_search/src/connectors/vibe.rs

WHAT IS VIBE: A Mistral-based coding assistant that stores session logs as JSONL.

SESSION FORMAT:
  Location: ~/.vibe/logs/session/{session-id}/messages.jsonl
  Env override: VIBE_HOME
  Directory structure: Each session gets its own subdirectory named by session ID.
  Each JSONL line: {"role":"user|assistant|system","content":"...","timestamp":"..."}

KEY DISTINGUISHING FEATURE — FLEXIBLE FIELD EXTRACTION:
  Vibe's JSONL isn't perfectly consistent. CASS handles this with fallback chains:
  - Role: Try "role" field → fallback to "speaker" → fallback to nested "message.role"
  - Content: Try "content" → fallback to "text" → fallback to nested "message.content"
  - Timestamp: Try "timestamp" → "created_at" → "createdAt" → "time" → "ts"
  This flexibility is the main complexity. Implement it carefully.

PROVIDER TRAIT IMPLEMENTATION:
  - slug: "vibe"
  - cli_alias: "vib"
  - name: "Vibe"
  - detect(): Check for ~/.vibe/logs/session/ directory (or VIBE_HOME)
  - session_roots(): Return [~/.vibe/logs/session/]
  - owns_session(id): Look for {id}/messages.jsonl or {id}/ directory in session roots
  - read_session(path): Parse messages.jsonl with flexible field extraction (role/content/timestamp fallbacks)
  - write_session(session, opts): Generate {session-id}/messages.jsonl with canonical field names. Use atomic_write.
  - resume_command(id): "vibe --resume {id}" (best guess)

CONSIDERATIONS:
  - Session ID comes from parent directory name, not filename
  - No workspace tracking (set workspace=None)
  - Title: first user message via truncate_title()
  - The flexible field extraction is the main value-add vs ClawdBot
  - No model tracking, no tool calls, no content blocks
  - Write path must create the session subdirectory

REFERENCE: CASS connector is ~130 lines. Follow ClawdBot pattern (once implemented) but add field fallbacks.

FILES TO CREATE/MODIFY:
  - CREATE: src/providers/vibe.rs
  - MODIFY: src/providers/mod.rs (add pub mod vibe)
  - MODIFY: src/discovery.rs (add to default_registry)
  - MODIFY: src/main.rs (update provider list in docs)
  - MODIFY: tests/json_contract_test.rs (update count + alias + VIBE_HOME env)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"CrimsonCanyon","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:05:28.388306288Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T03:22:30.870853186Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T03:22:30.870833069Z","close_reason":"Vibe provider implemented: flexible JSONL parsing, 15 unit tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-2d6","title":"Add CLI E2E tests for 6 newer providers","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T06:21:35.145115791Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T06:37:57.465791377Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T06:37:57.465767893Z","close_reason":"All 12 CLI E2E tests for 6 newer providers pass (ChatGPT, ClawdBot, Vibe, Factory, OpenClaw, PiAgent). Fixed AmbiguousSessionId with file-existence checks and --force flags. Fixed PiAgent alias (pi not pia). All quality gates green.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-2d7","title":"Add cross-provider test suite for all 14 providers (roundtrip, scalability, golden output)","description":"## Overview

After all 14 providers are implemented and wired, add comprehensive cross-provider tests that validate the full pipeline (read → canonical → write) works correctly for every provider combination.

This bead covers test infrastructure that spans multiple providers, NOT the per-provider unit tests (which are part of each provider's individual bead).

## Test Categories

### 1. Roundtrip Tests (`tests/roundtrip_test.rs`)
- For each provider: create synthetic native session → read → canonical → write → read again → compare
- Verify message count, roles, content, timestamps survive the roundtrip
- Test all 14 providers (currently tests exist for ~9)

### 2. Cross-Provider Pipeline Tests
- For each (source, target) pair where source != target:
  - Read source session → canonical → write to target → verify target file is valid
- Focus on the most common pairs: claude-code↔codex, gemini↔claude-code, etc.
- At minimum, test every new provider as both source and target against claude-code

### 3. Golden Output Tests (`tests/golden_output_test.rs`)
- For each new provider, add a golden input file under `tests/fixtures/` and a golden canonical output
- Verify that `read_session(golden_input)` produces exactly the golden canonical output
- This catches regressions in the reader logic

### 4. Scalability Tests (`tests/scalability_test.rs`)
- Verify that reading/writing large sessions (1000+ messages) works without excessive memory or time
- Test with at least one JSONL-based and one JSON-based provider

### 5. Discovery Tests (`tests/discovery_test.rs`)
- Test that `infer_provider_for_path()` correctly identifies all 14 providers
- Test ambiguous cases (e.g., JSONL files that could be multiple providers)
- Test the full `resolve_session()` flow for each new provider

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Roundtrip tests cover all 14 providers
- [ ] At least 5 cross-provider pipeline tests for new providers
- [ ] Golden output tests for all 5 new providers
- [ ] All tests pass, no regressions
- [ ] Total test count increases by 30+ from these new tests","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:09:54.326659226Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T03:32:46.346622386Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T03:32:46.346602969Z","close_reason":"Roundtrip tests added for all 6 new providers (10 tests), total 33 roundtrip tests passing across all 14 providers","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2d7","depends_on_id":"bd-352","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:11:35.163087383Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-2gb","title":"Add ergonomic shorthand resume flags and improve casr list UX","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-03T05:37:45.001416953Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-03T05:37:50.373064799Z","closed_at":"2026-03-03T05:37:50.373032358Z","close_reason":"Completed ergonomic flags (-cc/-cod/-gmi), styled workspace-scoped list defaults, and tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-2k0","title":"Fix unsound env var locking in integration tests (Rust 2024 unsafe set_var/remove_var)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-10T16:49:41.653759282Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T17:06:03.501751938Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T17:06:03.501732141Z","close_reason":"Implemented global re-entrant env lock for integration tests; removed per-provider Mutex scheme; fmt/clippy/tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-2m9","title":"Implement list_sessions() for Aider provider (multi-session history files)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T19:12:36.329684241Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T19:16:53.123418701Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T19:16:53.123400407Z","close_reason":"Implemented list_sessions() for Aider — enumerates all sessions by reading history files and splitting on session headers. Added test. 431 lib tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-2rl","title":"EPIC: Canonical Session Model","description":"Define the canonical intermediate representation (IR) for coding agent sessions. This is the Rosetta Stone of casr: every provider's native format is normalized into this IR, and every target format is generated from it.

Port/adapt the conversion-relevant data types and helper logic from CASS (coding_agent_session_search), but keep the IR focused on *session conversion*, not CASS's indexing/search features.

The canonical model must capture:
- ordered messages with roles (user/assistant/tool/system)
- timestamps (normalized to epoch millis)
- workspace/cwd when available
- session identity (source id and generated target id)
- model name (session-level convenience + message-level author)
- tool calls/results when representable
- provider-specific passthrough fields (lossless preservation via `session.metadata` and `message.extra`)

Intentional non-goals (do NOT pull in CASS-only indexing baggage):
- snippet extraction/indexing types (casr can derive summaries from message content without storing snippets as first-class IR fields)
- database ids / remote-origin fields

## Success Criteria

- `CanonicalSession`/`CanonicalMessage` are expressive enough to write plausible sessions for core providers.
- Provider-specific fields that cannot be canonically represented are preserved in `metadata`/`extra`.
- Timestamp and content normalization helpers are centralized and exhaustively unit-tested.
- IR choices are documented so writer implementations have clear mapping rules.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:36:44.447987709Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T05:41:43.484209368Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T05:41:43.484180534Z","close_reason":"All 3 children completed: CanonicalSession/CanonicalMessage types defined (bd-2rl.1), Provider trait defined (bd-2rl.2), all 5 content normalization helpers implemented (bd-2rl.3). The canonical IR is complete and ready for readers/writers to use.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","model"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2rl","depends_on_id":"bd-2zw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:46:04.457331048Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

@@ -40,6 +40,11 @@ casr info 019c3eae-94c3-7d73-9b2a-9edb18f1563b
 # 4) Convert that session to Claude Code format
 casr cc resume 019c3eae-94c3-7d73-9b2a-9edb18f1563b
 
+# ergonomic shorthand (auto-detects source provider from the session ID)
+casr -cc 019c3eae-94c3-7d73-9b2a-9edb18f1563b   # open in Claude Code
+casr -cod 019c3eae-94c3-7d73-9b2a-9edb18f1563b  # open in Codex
+casr -gmi 019c3eae-94c3-7d73-9b2a-9edb18f1563b  # open in Gemini CLI
+
 # 5) Resume in Claude Code using the generated ID
 claude --resume <new-session-id>
 ```
@@ -192,6 +197,9 @@ casr list
 casr list --provider codex
 casr list --workspace /data/projects/myapp
 casr list --limit 100 --sort messages
+
+# default behavior (no args): current workspace only, top 10, styled table output
+casr list
 ```
 
 ### `casr info <session-id>`

@@ -30,10 +30,13 @@ set -euo pipefail
 
 SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
 PROJECT_ROOT="$(cd "$SCRIPT_DIR/.." && pwd)"
-CARGO_TARGET="${CARGO_TARGET_DIR:-$PROJECT_ROOT/target}"
-CASR="${CASR_BIN:-$CARGO_TARGET/debug/casr}"
+# Always default to the project-local debug binary so smoke runs match the
+# current source tree even when CARGO_TARGET_DIR points elsewhere.
+CASR="${CASR_BIN:-$PROJECT_ROOT/target/debug/casr}"
 VERBOSE="${VERBOSE:-0}"
 SMOKE_ACCEPT_TIMEOUT="${SMOKE_ACCEPT_TIMEOUT:-8}"
+SMOKE_WORKSPACE="${SMOKE_WORKSPACE:-/data/projects}"
+SMOKE_REBUILD="${SMOKE_REBUILD:-1}"
 
 timestamp="$(date -u +%Y%m%dT%H%M%SZ)"
 ARTIFACTS_DIR="${SMOKE_ARTIFACTS_DIR:-$PROJECT_ROOT/artifacts/real-smoke/$timestamp}"
@@ -205,9 +208,13 @@ ensure_prereqs() {
         echo "ERROR: timeout is required."
         exit 1
     fi
-    if [[ ! -x "$CASR" ]]; then
+    if [[ "$SMOKE_REBUILD" == "1" || ! -x "$CASR" ]]; then
         banner "Building casr"
-        (cd "$PROJECT_ROOT" && cargo build --quiet)
+        if command -v rch > /dev/null 2>&1; then
+            (cd "$PROJECT_ROOT" && rch exec -- cargo build --quiet)
+        else
+            (cd "$PROJECT_ROOT" && cargo build --quiet)
+        fi
     fi
     if [[ ! -x "$CASR" ]]; then
         echo "ERROR: casr binary not found at $CASR"
@@ -219,8 +226,52 @@ source_session_for_alias() {
     local alias="$1"
     local slug="${PROVIDER_SLUG[$alias]}"
     local prefix="$ARTIFACTS_DIR/list_${alias}"
+    local candidate_file=""
+    local candidate_id=""
+    local uuid_re='^[0-9a-fA-F]{8}-[0-9a-fA-F]{4}-[0-9a-fA-F]{4}-[0-9a-fA-F]{4}-[0-9a-fA-F]{12}$'
+
+    case "$alias" in
+        cc)
+            candidate_file="$(find "${PROVIDER_HOME[$alias]}/projects" -type f -name '*.jsonl' -printf '%T@ %p\n' 2>/dev/null | sort -nr 2>/dev/null | head -n 1 | cut -d' ' -f2- || true)"
+            if [[ -n "$candidate_file" ]]; then
+                # Prefer authoritative sessionId from file body.
+                candidate_id="$(jq -r '.sessionId // empty' "$candidate_file" 2>/dev/null | head -n 1)"
+                if [[ -z "$candidate_id" ]]; then
+                    candidate_id="$(basename "$candidate_file" .jsonl)"
+                fi
+                # Ignore non-session UUID-like filenames (e.g. compact artifacts).
+                if [[ ! "$candidate_id" =~ $uuid_re ]]; then
+                    candidate_id=""
+                fi
+            fi
+            ;;
+        cod)
+            candidate_file="$(find "${PROVIDER_HOME[$alias]}/sessions" -type f -name '*.jsonl' -printf '%T@ %p\n' 2>/dev/null | sort -nr 2>/dev/null | head -n 1 | cut -d' ' -f2- || true)"
+            if [[ -n "$candidate_file" ]]; then
+                candidate_id="$(jq -r 'select(.type=="session_meta") | .payload.id // empty' "$candidate_file" 2>/dev/null | head -n 1)"
+            fi
+            ;;
+        gmi)
+            candidate_file="$(find "${PROVIDER_HOME[$alias]}/tmp" -type f -name 'session-*.json' -printf '%T@ %p\n' 2>/dev/null | sort -nr 2>/dev/null | head -n 1 | cut -d' ' -f2- || true)"
+            if [[ -n "$candidate_file" ]]; then
+                # Prefer full sessionId from file body over filename timestamp prefix.
+                candidate_id="$(jq -r '.sessionId // empty' "$candidate_file" 2>/dev/null | head -n 1)"
+                if [[ -z "$candidate_id" ]]; then
+                    candidate_id="$(basename "$candidate_file" .json)"
+                    candidate_id="${candidate_id#session-}"
+                fi
+            fi
+            ;;
+    esac
 
-    run_cmd "$prefix" "$CASR" --json list --provider "$slug" --limit 25
+    if [[ -n "$candidate_id" ]]; then
+        log "Resolved source session for $alias via fast path: $candidate_id ($candidate_file)"
+        echo "$candidate_id"
+        return 0
+    fi
+
+    # Fallback: ask casr directly, but bound runtime to avoid full-home scans hanging forever.
+    run_cmd "$prefix" timeout 25s "$CASR" --json list --provider "$slug" --workspace "$SMOKE_WORKSPACE" --limit 25
     if [[ "$LAST_EXIT" -ne 0 ]]; then
         echo ""
         return 0
@@ -267,6 +318,13 @@ expand_accept_command() {
     local resume_command="$3"
     local template="${ACCEPT_TEMPLATE[$alias]}"
     if [[ -z "$template" ]]; then
+        # Codex CLI requires a TTY; wrap with `script` when available so
+        # acceptance probes exercise real resume behavior instead of failing
+        # with "stdin is not a terminal".
+        if [[ "$alias" == "cod" ]] && command -v script > /dev/null 2>&1; then
+            echo "script -q -c 'codex resume $target_session' /dev/null"
+            return 0
+        fi
         echo "$resume_command"
         return 0
     fi
